title: Acceptance Self-Eval Verdict
description: Structured output of one independent (fresh-context) acceptance self-eval round (Story #3819). Produced by the critic pass in the Story-implementation phase (helpers/deliver-story.md Step 1a), written under the gitignored temp tree, and consumed by acceptance-eval.js to enforce the bounded round cap and decide whether the Story proceeds to `closing`, redrafts, or escalates to `agent::blocked`. The author MUST NOT be the evaluator — the verdict is an independent judgment of the caller-injected change set against each inline acceptance[] item.

storyId.description: GitHub Issue number of the Story being evaluated.

epicId.description: Parent Epic id when the Story is Epic-attached; null for standalone Stories.

schemaVersion.description: Verdict-record schema version. Bumped on breaking changes to the record shape.

round.description: 1-based index of this eval round. Round 1 is the first evaluation after implementation; rounds 2..N are post-redraft re-evaluations. Bounded by delivery.acceptanceEval.maxRounds (clamped to an undisableable hard ceiling).

commitSha.description: Output of `git rev-parse HEAD` at the moment the diff was evaluated. Optional but recommended for traceability.

criteria.description: One verdict per inline acceptance[] item, in the same order as the Story body. Every acceptance item MUST appear exactly once.

index.description: 0-based position of this criterion in the Story's acceptance[] array.

criterion.description: The verbatim (or faithfully summarised) acceptance[] item text being scored.

verdict.description: Per-criterion judgment of the caller-injected change set. `met` clears the criterion; `partial` and `unmet` both trigger a redraft round (subject to the cap).

evidence.description: Short concrete evidence supporting the verdict — file:line references, test names, command output excerpts. Never an empty self-attestation.

verifyEvidence.description: Required evidence drawn from the Story's verify[] commands the critic ran (or inspected) while scoring this criterion. verify[] is promoted from optional advisory pre-flight to required evidence the eval pass consumes. Empty only when no verify[] command is relevant to this criterion.

command.description: The verify[] command that was run or inspected.

outcome.description: Result of the verify[] command. `skipped` is permitted only with a stated reason and never for a criterion marked `met`.

detail.description: Optional excerpt of command output or the reason a command was skipped.
